archimate:BusinessObject.name: Data Governance Guidelines
archimate:BusinessObject.documentation:
Definition: Data Governance Guidelines can be defined as a set of principles, rules, and practices that provide direction and guidance for managing and controlling data assets within an organization. These guidelines outline the framework for ensuring data quality, integrity, privacy, security, and compliance throughout the data lifecycle, including data collection, storage, usage, and sharing.
Source: Data Governance Institute
Source reference: https://datagovernance.com/goals-and-principles-for-data-governance/
LOST view: SV-Governance Architecture Principles
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/DataGovernanceGuidelinesBusinessObject
ABB name: egovera:DataGovernanceGuidelinesBusinessObject
EIRA concept: eira:ArchitectureBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2023-11-20
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/DataGovernanceGuidelinesBusinessObject
Interoperability Layer: Semantic
Specialises: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/DataPolicy

properties.value: Data Governance Guidelines can be defined as a set of principles, rules, and practices that provide direction and guidance for managing and controlling data assets within an organization. These guidelines outline the framework for ensuring data quality, integrity, privacy, security, and compliance throughout the data lifecycle, including data collection, storage, usage, and sharing.
